title: cerastium latifolium subsp. pyrenaicum
intro: Cerastium latifolium subsp. pyrenaicum, also known as Pyrenean mouse-ear, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family that is native to the Pyrenees mountains between France and Spain. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help soothe wounds and reduce fever. The health benefits of Cerastium latifolium subsp. pyrenaicum include its ability to support the immune system and reduce inflammation, making it a potential remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into infusions or tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
